outcome: Resolve urgent customer cases accurately with fewer repeated actions
observations: Each case requires separate open, resolve, and confirmation actions.
Urgency is repeated through color, text, and a dedicated status column.

lenses.diagnosis: The queue is visually moderate and not the primary constraint.
Status is communicated redundantly while actionable age is understated.
Urgency and ownership are separated despite being read together during triage.
Repeated confirmation creates avoidable time cost for low-risk resolutions.
The highest value change is faster safe resolution, not tighter row spacing.

recommendations.change: Offer a reversible bulk resolution action for cases with the same low-risk disposition.
recommendations.expectedValue: Support leads can clear routine work while preserving attention for urgent exceptions.
recommendations.tradeoff: Batch actions can amplify mistakes if unlike cases are grouped.
recommendations.verification: Measure completion time and reversal rate on a representative fixture set.

constraints: Preserve readable type and accessible target sizes.
Require confirmation when a resolution is irreversible or affects unlike cases.
preferences: Prefer compact operational layouts when hierarchy remains clear.
uncertainties: No production timing or error-rate data was supplied.
